description.p: Colophon turns KOReader's reading statistics into attractive, varied graphs and widgets: a native GTK4 desktop app with its own flat, hard-edged design, not a web dashboard or a self-hosted service.
It reads a copy of the device's statistics database and never touches the original, then presents both a library-wide overview and per-book detail.

ul.li: An All Books overview: totals, streaks, a year heatmap, and a when-do-I-read grid
Per-book stat cards that match the numbers KOReader shows on the device
Reading-speed trends, session analytics, and a per-page activity strip
Local-first: no accounts, no telemetry, no cloud, fully offline

description.p: Two new overview charts, computed from the statistics database alone: a speed-by-hour-of-day bar chart (pages per hour by clock hour, a companion to the speed trend), and a cumulative reading-time curve that plots total time read across your whole history.

Fixes a visual glitch where pressing a bare modifier key flashed the accent colour across the whole window: the keyboard focus ring is now drawn only on interactive controls, not on every widget in the focus chain.

Colophon's look is now entirely its own. libadwaita is gone: plain GTK4 widgets, an app-generated stylesheet per palette (flat, square, 1px borders, no shadows), a slim toolbar without window buttons, a manual F9 sidebar toggle with a persisted width, and follow-system dark and light read directly from the desktop settings portal. All statistics, imports, themes, and sidecar features are unchanged.

Device auto-pull: Colophon re-imports automatically at launch and when the device is mounted, refreshing the statistics database and any attached sidecars from the exact paths you previously provided. Still read-only, still copy-based.
First stable release. The full widget catalogue, per-book sidecar reconciliation, eight themes, and Meson and Flatpak packaging are all shipped.
